ads.author: Steven Moritz
ads.title: (PENDING) Williams Star Wars Episode I Pinball Machine – Excellent Condition – LED Upgraded
ads.description: For sale is a Williams / Bally Star Wars Episode I pinball machine in excellent condition. Released in 1999, this platform is commonly referred to as Pin2k as the playfield can be swapped with other games in the Pin2k series. This is a fun and unique machine based on the hit film Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace, featuring fast gameplay, interactive toys, and the iconic holographic playfield display. The game plays great and has been well cared for in a smoke-free home. Features: Interactive hologram display with moving characters Widebody playfield with multiple ramps and targets LED lighting upgrades Strong flippers and smooth gameplay Cabinet and playfield in great shape Clean inside and out Transformer in light saber recently replaced This machine was produced by Williams Electronics, one of the most respected names in pinball, and is a great addition for collectors or anyone wanting a fun centerpiece for their game room. Condition: Fully working and ready to play. Normal cosmetic wear consistent with age.

Original artwork in good condition, though one side is slightly faded (typical for a machine of this age). Cabinet has normal age-related wear, but overall very clean. Recently cleaned and serviced – plays smooth and fast. Includes original manual and keys Gameplay Highlights: Help Gilligan and the gang escape the island by collecting ingredients to appease the fire god! Lighthearted theme music, colorful tropical graphics, and classic Bally build quality make this a ton of fun to play. Local pickup preferred (local delivery possible). Message me if you want to try it out or see more pictures and video of it running — this one’s ready to play and enjoy!

This was put together in 2018 using a New Old Stock (NOS) conversion kit with fresh artwork, along with a NOS monitor for an event with Billy Mitchell at the Southern-Fried Gaming Expo in Atlanta. The monitor probably has fewer than 200 hours on it since it was constructed. The sides are new, smooth black laminate. You can see a couple of tears in the side art that happened during transport, but otherwise a perfect specimen. Ground-level pickup at my home in Gainesville, GA 30506. I'm asking $1500 OBO and open to trades.
